data.title: Bitcoin Tests US$75,000, Ethereum Holds Firm As Crypto Momentum Builds
data.description:
The crypto market extended its rebound on April 15, with Bitcoin hovering near key breakout levels while Ethereum showed resilience above critical support, signalling renewed bullish momentum despite geopolitical volatility.

Bitcoin traded just below the US$75,000 mark after briefly pushing higher in recent sessions, as fresh capital inflows and improving risk appetite lifted sentiment across digital assets.

Analysts say the flagship cryptocurrency is now flirting with a decisive technical breakout, one that could open the door to a move toward the US$90,000-US$98,000 range if sustained.

Ethereum, meanwhile, steadied above US$2,300, reinforcing its role as a relative laggard but stable anchor in the current rally. The world’s second-largest token has benefited from the same wave of inflows, though its upside remains more measured compared to Bitcoin’s surge.

The broader rally comes as over US$1 billion in fresh capital flowed into crypto markets, underscoring a shift back toward risk assets even as macro uncertainty lingers.

Still, volatility remains close beneath the surface. Prices have been whipsawed in recent days by developments tied to the Middle East conflict, with energy shocks and shifting risk sentiment increasingly driving crypto’s short-term direction, marking a departure from its traditionally more independent narrative.

For now, traders are watching one key question: Can Bitcoin decisively clear resistance and lead the next leg higher or will geopolitical turbulence stall the rally just as momentum builds?

data.title: Stock Picks: IGB REIT And Vitrox
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has flagged IGB REIT Bhd and Vitrox Bhd as near-term trading ideas, citing strengthening bullish momentum supported by technical breakouts on higher volumes.

RHB Research said IGB REIT has extended its upward trajectory after breaking above the RM2.80 resistance level, supported by a bullish candlestick formation that signals strengthening positive momentum. The research house noted that the breakout could see the stock advance towards RM3.00, with a further upside target at RM3.20 if buying interest persists. However, it cautioned that a decline below the RM2.60 support level may trigger a correction.

Meanwhile, Vitrox is seen to be continuing its uptrend after surging past the RM4.75 resistance on strong volume, reinforcing market confidence in the counter’s bullish setup. RHB Research said sustained momentum could lift the stock towards RM5.10 in the near term, followed by a higher resistance level at RM5.35. On the downside, a pullback below RM4.50 may indicate a potential retracement.

The research house highlighted that both counters are showing constructive technical patterns, with breakout movements suggesting continued upside potential, although investors are advised to monitor key support levels for signs of trend reversal.

data.title: Bursa Slips At Midday As Geopolitical Jitters Hit Blue Chips
data.description:
The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) erased earlier gains to trade lower by midday of April 15, as investor caution set in ahead of a ceasefire deadline between the US and Iran, triggering selling in heavyweight counters.

At 12:30pm, the benchmark index fell 5.63 points, or 0.33%, to 1,682.49 after climbing as high as 1,695.68 earlier in the session.

The pullback was led by losses in key index-linked names, including Petronas Gas Bhd, Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd, Tenaga Nasional Bhd and MISC Bhd, reflecting profit-taking and risk-off sentiment.

Meanwhile, broader indices painted a mixed picture. The FBM 70 rose 116.39 points (0.67%) to 17,492.65, indicating continued interest in smaller-cap stocks, while the FBM Emas edged slightly lower at 12,401.29 after losing 4.96 points (0.04%).

Despite the softer index, broader market sentiment remained constructive. Gainers outpaced losers 568 to 392, pointing to selective buying in mid- and small-cap stocks.

Among the top performers, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd led the gainers, followed by Hong Leong Industries Bhd and Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd, signalling pockets of strength in industrial and consumer names.

Market activity remained brisk, with turnover reaching 1.97 billion shares valued at RM1.4 billion. Actively traded counters included newly ACE Market debutant 5E Resources Bhd, MMAG Holdings Bhd and AirAsia X Bhd.

For now, traders are expected to remain cautious, with geopolitical developments, particularly the outcome of US-Iran ceasefire talks, likely to dictate near-term market direction.

data.title: Bursa Opens Higher, Broader Market Stays Positive As Oil Eases On US-Iran Peace Hopes
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened slightly higher, tracking firmer regional sentiment and easing crude oil prices as markets priced in potential progress in US-Iran peace negotiations that could reduce geopolitical risk in the Strait of Hormuz.

The FBMKLCI rose 2.58 points or 0.15% to 1,686.00 in early trade. Broader indices also edged higher, with the FBM70 up 66.68 points or 0.38% to 17,569.95, while the FBMEMAS gained 27.64 points or 0.22% to 12,436.90.

The FBMSHA advanced 36.21 points or 0.29% to 12,368.28, while the F4GBM index added 2.25 points or 0.22% to 1,003.47, reflecting a generally positive tone across the market.

Sentiment was supported by Wall Street’s overnight gains, where US equities hit fresh records as investors grew more optimistic that tensions between Washington and Tehran could ease, helping to stabilise energy markets.

Oil prices eased in early trade after reports suggested Iran could allow freer navigation through parts of the Strait of Hormuz under a potential agreement, easing fears of prolonged supply disruptions in a route that handles around 20% of global oil flows.

On the broader market, advance-decline figures showed a positive bias, with 197 gainers against 111 decliners, suggesting underlying buying interest despite cautious trading at the index level.

Trading volume stood at 297.44 million shares with a value of S$329 million.

Among actively traded counters, MMAG was unchanged at 0.035 with 625,878 lots traded, while GD Group rose 0.025 to 0.475. AAX added 0.020 to 1.260, while Velesto was flat at 0.330.

On the blue-chip front, gains were seen in UMS International, which rose 0.17 or 9.50% to 1.960. Nestlé gained 0.480 to 99.480, Allianz added 0.220 to 21.000 and MBM Resources climbed 0.220 to 5.100.

Decliners included PChem, which fell 0.130 to 5.500, CIMB down 0.080 to 7.420, PetDag easing 0.120 to 20.600, Heineken slipping 0.120 to 23.060 and MPI down 0.160 to 32.840.

In the banking space, DBS traded at 57.320, OCBC at 22.770 and UOB at 37.490, while Singtel was at 4.890. SGX was last seen at 21.050.

Market participants noted that while sentiment has improved on expectations of de-escalation in the Middle East, volatility may persist as investors continue to monitor developments around US-Iran talks and their potential impact on global energy flows and inflation.

data.title: Wall Street Rallies On Tech Strength And Easing Oil Concerns
data.description:
US stocks closed at fresh record highs, with investors brushing aside lingering concerns over the Iran conflict as optimism around earnings and potential peace talks drove renewed buying in technology shares.

The S&P 500 rose 0.8% to end at 7,022.95, surpassing its previous record, while the Nasdaq Composite climbed 1.6% to 24,016.02, also marking a new all-time high. Both indices also touched intraday records during the session.

The latest gains extended a two-day rally that has pushed major US benchmarks to levels not seen since late January, with the Nasdaq reaching its highest close since October last year.

Market sentiment was supported by expectations that tensions between the United States and Iran could ease, helping to reduce the risk of further disruption to global energy markets. Oil prices have retreated in recent sessions as traders price in a possible de-escalation in the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route handling about 20% of global oil flows.

Investors also reacted positively to the early stages of the corporate earnings season, with stronger-than-expected results helping to reinforce confidence in corporate profitability and the broader economic outlook.

Despite the rally, analysts noted that uncertainty remains around the geopolitical situation, with markets still sensitive to developments in ongoing negotiations between Washington and Tehran.

On Wall Street, the advance was led by technology stocks, which continued to attract strong risk-on flows after recent volatility. Broader participation across sectors also supported the move, with traders pointing to resilient economic data and easing inflation pressures as additional tailwinds.

Several market participants said the recent rebound reflects a combination of improving fundamentals and the view that worst-case geopolitical scenarios may be less likely than previously feared.

Still, caution remains in the background as investors monitor whether diplomatic momentum can translate into a lasting resolution, particularly given the potential impact on energy prices and global inflation dynamics.

Reuters

data.title: SGX Opens Lower As Oil Softens On US-Iran Peace Hopes
data.description:
Singapore shares opened slightly lower as investors reacted to easing oil prices and mixed global sentiment driven by renewed optimism over US-Iran peace talks.

As at 9.27am, the Straits Times Index (STI) fell 9.52 points or 0.19% to 5,011.68. Market breadth was positive overall with 197 gainers versus 111 decliners, while early turnover stood at 297.44 million shares valued at S$329 million.

Sentiment across Asia was shaped by a softer tone in crude oil markets, where Brent crude slipped 0.5% to US$94.49 a barrel and US West Texas Intermediate declined 0.8% to US$90.59. The pullback came as investors priced in the possibility of de-escalation in the Strait of Hormuz following reports of potential diplomatic progress between Washington and Tehran.

Market watchers noted that optimism over a possible agreement has helped ease immediate supply disruption fears, although uncertainty remains over whether negotiations will lead to a lasting resolution given the strategic importance of the region, which handles about 20% of global oil flows.

Wall Street’s overnight performance provided a more constructive backdrop, with US stocks closing at fresh record highs as tech-led gains overshadowed geopolitical concerns. The rally was supported by strong corporate earnings and expectations that easing energy pressures could help stabilise inflation.

Back in Singapore, banking stocks were broadly steady in early trade. DBS traded at S$57.32, OCBC Bank at S$22.77 and UOB at S$37.49. Singtel was at S$4.89 while SGX changed hands at S$21.05.

Among active counters, UMS stood out with a 9.5% jump to S$1.96, while YZJ Shipbuilding eased to S$4.05.

Regional markets are expected to remain sensitive to developments in US-Iran negotiations and fluctuations in oil prices, with traders balancing improving risk appetite against lingering geopolitical uncertainty.

data.title: Bursa Slips At Close As Profit-Taking Hits Financials, Utilities
data.description:
FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) reversed early gains to end slightly lower on April 15, as profit-taking in financial and utility heavyweights dragged the benchmark into negative territory.

The FBM KLCI fell 4.70 points, or 0.28%, to 1,683.42, after earlier touching an intraday high of 1,695.68.

Despite the softer headline index, broader market sentiment remained resilient. The FBM 70 rose 127.01 points (0.73%) to 17,503.27, while the FBM Emas and FBM Shariah indices also ended marginally higher, pointing to continued buying interest in mid- and small-cap stocks.

However, losses in key index-linked stocks weighed on the market. Public Bank Bhd slipped four sen, while CIMB Group Holdings Bhd and IHH Healthcare Bhd each declined five sen. Tenaga Nasional Bhd dropped 18 sen, offsetting modest gains in Malayan Banking Bhd, which edged up two sen.

Trading activity stayed active, led by 5E Resources Bhd, MMAG Holdings Bhd and Zetrix AI Bhd among the most actively traded counters.

The session reflects a market still in consolidation mode, with investors rotating out of heavyweights while selectively accumulating smaller-cap names amid cautious sentiment.

data.title: Asian Tech Stocks Surge As Nvidia’s Quantum AI Push Ignites Rally
data.description:
Asian software and IT stocks jumped sharply after Nvidia unveiled a new suite of open-source artificial intelligence (AI) models designed to accelerate breakthroughs in quantum computing, reigniting investor enthusiasm across the region’s tech sector.

In South Korea, shares of Axgate Co and ICTK Co briefly hit their daily 30% trading limits, leading a broad rally in software and cybersecurity names.

The momentum spilled across Asia, as China’s GuoChuang Software Co. and QuantumCTek Co. each climbed more than 8%, while Japan’s Fixstars Corp also posted strong gains.

At the centre of the rally is Nvidia’s new Ising AI model, which aims to tackle complex optimisation problems tied to quantum systems, fuelling fresh optimism that artificial intelligence could help make quantum computing scalable and commercially viable.

The announcement has amplified expectations that AI may act as a bridge technology, accelerating progress in a field long viewed as transformative but still largely experimental.

Beyond the tech catalyst, improving macro sentiment also played a role. Regional markets found additional support from signs that the US and Iran were reviving peace talks, easing geopolitical tensions that had weighed on risk assets.

The renewed interest comes against a backdrop of strong long-term growth expectations. The global quantum computing market is projected to expand from about US$1.7 billion in 2024 to over US$11 billion by 2030, underscoring the scale of opportunity investors are positioning for.

For now, Nvidia’s latest move has done more than just push technological boundaries, it has reignited a powerful narrative: That the convergence of AI and quantum computing could define the next frontier of innovation.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Higher Against Dollar Amid US-Iran Talks
data.description:
The ringgit opened marginally higher against the US dollar today, supported by optimism that renewed US-Iran talks could ease tensions along key global oil routes, per Bernama.

At 8 am, the local currency edged up to 3.9510/9570 against the greenback, from 3.9550/9600 at yesterday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said markets were pinning hopes on a second round of US-Iran talks delivering an outcome that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz and further de-escalate the conflict.

He added that inflation concerns, driven by war-related pressures on US firms and rising US Treasury yields, would keep borrowing costs elevated, as the US Federal Reserve may be reluctant to cut interest rates.

“Expect ringgit versus the US dollar to remain in a narrow range of between RM3.95 and RM3.97 today,” he told Bernama.

At press time, West Texas Intermediate and Brent crude prices had both fallen below US$100 per barrel, easing 0.58 per cent and 0.36 per cent to US$90.76 and US$94.59, respectively.

At the opening, the ringgit traded mixed against a basket of major currencies.

It weakened against the British pound to 5.3627/3708 from 5.3614/3682 at Wednesday’s close, slipped versus the euro to 4.6649/6720 from 4.6594/6653, but strengthened against the Japanese yen to 2.4879/4918 from 2.4890/4923.

Meanwhile, the local currency traded mostly higher against its ASEAN peers.

It edged up against the Singapore dollar to 3.1093/1143 from 3.1095/1139 on Wednesday, improved versus the Indonesian rupiah to 230.4/230.9 from 230.7/231.0, and rose against the Philippine peso to 6.57/6.59 from 6.58/6.59 previously.

However, the ringgit weakened against the Thai baht to 12.3415/3676 from 12.3098/3330 yesterday.

data.title: Wall Street Surges As Easing US-Iran Tensions And Earnings Strength Lift Sentiment
data.description:
US equities rallied strongly on Monday as renewed hopes for US-Iran talks and a solid start to the earnings season boosted investor risk appetite, according to Reuters.

The Nasdaq Composite jumped 1.96% to 23,639.08, extending its winning streak to ten consecutive sessions, while the S&P 500 gained 1.18% to 6,967.38, closing just shy of its record high. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.66% to 48,535.99, marking its strongest finish since early March.

Market sentiment was supported by reports that talks to end the Iran conflict could resume in Pakistan within days, alongside developments suggesting potential diplomatic progress in the Middle East. Investors also reacted positively to US inflation data showing producer prices rose less than expected in March, reinforcing expectations of a resilient economic backdrop.

“We don’t have a resolution yet but investors don’t want to miss the rebound,” said Burns McKinney, portfolio manager at NFJ Investment Group.

Analysts noted that easing geopolitical fears have helped markets move past peak uncertainty, with participants increasingly buying dips amid improved valuations and earnings momentum.

Sector performance was mixed, with energy stocks declining 2.2% as oil prices fell, while technology led gains. Software stocks rose 1.6% and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index climbed 2% to a fifth consecutive record close.

On the earnings front, BlackRock shares rose 3% after reporting higher first-quarter profit driven by ETF inflows and performance fees, while Citigroup advanced 2.6% to its highest level since 2008 after beating earnings expectations. Wells Fargo fell after missing interest income forecasts, while JPMorgan saw a more muted reaction.

Elsewhere, airline stocks surged after reports of potential industry consolidation discussions, with American Airlines jumping 8% and United Airlines gaining 2%.

Overall, advancing stocks outpaced decliners by more than two to one on both the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq, reflecting broad-based buying momentum across US equity markets.

data.title: Selective Buying Lifts Gainers As Market Movement Stays Positive At Close
data.description:
Gains in selected heavyweights led by Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd (MPI) and Malayan Cement Bhd lifted sentiment on Bursa Malaysia, with gainers outpacing losers at the close despite pockets of selling in key index-linked stocks.

MPI topped the gainers list, jumping RM2.36 to RM33, followed by Malayan Cement, which rose 31 sen to RM6.91. Consumer name Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd added 30 sen to RM30.10, while Hong Leong Industries Bhd and ITMAX System Bhd also posted solid gains at 30 sen and 25 sen to close their trading session at RM17.04 and RM4.94, respectively.

Market breadth remained positive, with 615 gainers outpacing 455 decliners. A total of 566 counters were unchanged, while 1,057 stocks were untraded and 13 suspended.

On the downside, selling pressure persisted in energy and utility names. Petronas Gas Bhd led the losers, shedding 36 sen to RM17.90, followed by Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd, down 27 sen to RM5.63. MISC Bhd and Tenaga Nasional Bhd also declined, reflecting continued rotation out of defensive heavyweights.

Overall, the session pointed to selective buying interest, particularly in industrial and tech-linked counters, even as broader market sentiment remained cautious.

data.title: Stock Today: Hibiscus Petroleum Slips 1.39% Amid Elevated Crude Oil Prices
data.description:
Hibiscus Petroleum Bhd was trading lower, as the stock came off earlier highs amid profit taking despite a broadly supportive oil price backdrop driven by continued geopolitical tensions.

As at 12.19 pm, Hibiscus was last done at RM2.13, down 3 sen or 1.39% with 56.36 million shares traded. The counter opened at RM2.11, touched an intraday high of RM2.17 and a low of RM2.09, with buying interest seen at RM2.13 against selling pressure at RM2.14.

The weaker tone comes even as broader sector sentiment remains underpinned by elevated crude oil prices, with Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd maintaining a BUY call on Hibiscus Petroleum and an unchanged target price of RM2.74. Analysts said the stock continues to be supported by expectations of stronger realised oil prices and higher lifting volumes in the second half of FY26.

HLIB noted that global oil markets remain structurally tight following significant supply disruptions in the Middle East, with estimates indicating about 44% of crude output from key producers has been taken offline since the onset of the Iran conflict. Analysts added that Brent crude has surged more than 60% since late February, at one point nearing US$120 per barrel, reinforcing a higher-for-longer price environment.

Despite near-term volatility, analysts said Southeast Asia’s upstream sector is positioned for longer-term gains as tighter global supply conditions could drive increased exploration activity and renewed interest from international and national oil companies. They added that this could support regional positioning as an alternative supply hub.

For Hibiscus, analysts expect earnings recovery in 2HFY26 as production normalises following earlier maintenance-related disruptions, with higher Brent-linked realised prices expected to provide direct earnings uplift. HLIB also raised its FY26 dividend expectations to 10 sen from 7 sen, citing stronger oil price assumptions and improved cash flow visibility.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Closes Higher As Key Indices Advance
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended higher on April 14, with key indices closing in positive territory amid improved market sentiment.

The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBMKLCI) rose 7.60 points, or 0.45%, to close at 1,688.12.

Broader market indices also recorded gains, with the FBM70 climbing 179.41 points, or 1.04%, to 17,376.26, while the F4GBM index increased 5.83 points, or 0.59%, to 1,001.64.

Meanwhile, the FBMEMAS index advanced 72.98 points, or 0.59%, to 12,406.25, and the FBMSHA index gained 45.53 points, or 0.37%, to settle at 12,312.59.

 Among the most actively traded stocks, Velesto Energy Bhd fell 2 sen to 32.5 sen with 965.09 million shares traded, while MMAG Holdings Bhd was unchanged at 4 sen, recording 703.28 million shares.

Other active stock were Zetrix AI Bhd, Bumi Armada Bhd, and, VS Industry Bhd.


The overall market performance reflects steady buying interest across segments, with broader indices outperforming the benchmark.

data.title: HSIF Seen Consolidating Near 26,000 Level, RHB Maintains Short Bias
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained a negative trading bias on the Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) and advised traders to retain short positions, even as the index showed signs of near-term consolidation around the 26,000-point level.

The research house said HSIF gained 251 points to close at 25,916 points after testing the immediate resistance at 26,000 points during intraday trade. It added that the index fluctuated between a low of 25,572 points and a high of 26,024 points before paring gains, while the evening session saw a further 229-point increase to 26,145 points.

According to RHB Research, the price action suggests the index is attempting to build an interim base near 26,000 points, with selling pressure easing and movement shifting into a sideways consolidation phase.

“Resorting to sideways movements,” RHB Research noted, adding that while there is potential for a further breakout above 26,000 points if momentum persists, the overall bias remains negative unless HSIF closes above 26,600 points.

The firm reiterated its recommendation for traders to hold short positions initiated on 26 February at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss level maintained at 26,600 points.

On the downside, RHB Research highlighted initial support at 24,500 points, followed by 23,500 points, while resistance levels are now seen at 26,000 points and 26,600 points respectively.

The research house said near-term price action will likely be driven by whether the index can sustain gains above the 26,000-point threshold or revert back into downside consolidation within its broader bearish structure.

data.title: US Stock Rise On Truce Talks, PPI Comes In Slower
data.description:
US stocks rose on Tuesday while oil prices fell after President Trump signaled he’s open to further talks with Iran, stoking optimism for a long-term truce.

The S&P 500 edged up 0.3%, while the Nasdaq Composite gained a stronger 0.7%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, which includes fewer tech names, rose less than 0.1% above the flat line. On Monday, the Wall Street benchmarks eked out small wins after software stocks ripped higher.

Optimism that the April 7 truce can be extended before it expires next week has added to already growing hopes for a longer-lasting peace deal, which on Monday helped the S&P 500 effectively wipe out losses accumulated since the start of the conflict.

Against that backdrop, oil prices dropped back below $100 a barrel, but with investors watching for signs of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. West Texas Intermediate crude retreated 3.5% to trade near $96 per barrel, while Brent crude fell 2% to sit near around $97.30

US producer prices rose slower than expected in March, according to data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics released Tuesday, rising 0.5% in March over the previous month, in line with February’s revised gain of 0.5% and less than economists’ expectations for an increase of 1.1% on the month.

Yahoo Finance

data.title: Stock Today: Muhibbah Up 2% After Penang LRT Award Lifts Earnings Visibility
data.description:
Muhibbah Engineering (M) Bhd experienced a rise of 2.00%, closing at RM0.51 during mid-afternoon trading. The stock fluctuated between RM0.505 and RM0.52, with approximately 2.06 million shares traded.

This positive sentiment was attributed to new contract awards and a more optimistic earnings forecast from analysts, who maintained an “Add” recommendation with a target price of RM0.81. The analysts emphasized the enhancement in order book visibility and potential re-rating catalysts stemming from the recovery in the construction sector and tourism-related assets.

Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd maintained its positive stance on Muhibbah Engineering while CGS International Securities also reiterated an Add recommendation with a sum-of-parts target price of RM0.81, as analysts pointed to the group’s RM120 million Penang LRT noise barrier contract as a key boost to its construction pipeline and earnings visibility, alongside expectations of continued dividend yields of 5% to 7% through FY2026 to FY2028.

The RM120 million award for the design, supply, installation, testing and commissioning of noise barriers and enclosures for the Penang Light Rail Transit Mutiara Line project was secured from Gamuda Industrial Building System Sdn Bhd, with work scheduled to commence in April 2026 and targeted for completion by the third quarter of 2030, marking Muhibbah’s first construction win for FY2026 and reinforcing its niche in rail and infrastructure noise barrier works.

Analysts said the contract lifts Muhibbah’s construction orderbook to about RM983 million or around RM1.6 billion including its crane segment, accounting for roughly 30% of FY2026 new contract win forecasts, while gross profit margins are expected in the range of 8% to 9%, helping to offset earnings pressure from its Cambodia airport operations which have shifted to a management services model.

CGS International noted that despite improving fundamentals, the market is still assigning an overly pessimistic negative implied valuation to the construction division, even as Muhibbah’s Petronas-linked capabilities and track record in large-scale infrastructure projects continue to support longer-term earnings recovery potential and position the stock for a gradual re-rating as execution improves.

data.title: Stock Picks: Sunway And Gamuda
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd identified Sunway Bhd and Gamuda Bhd as near-term trading ideas, citing improving technical momentum following bullish breakouts supported by strong trading volumes.

RHB Research said Sunway has extended its upward trajectory after breaking above the RM5.20 resistance level, with the formation of a bullish candlestick signalling strengthening momentum. The research house expects the counter to potentially advance towards RM5.50, with further upside towards RM5.70 if buying interest persists. However, it noted that a decline below the RM5.00 support level could trigger a corrective move.

Gamuda was also highlighted after staging a breakout above the RM4.05 resistance level on strong volume, indicating growing bullish sentiment. RHB said the stock may see follow-through buying that could lift prices towards RM4.40, with the next resistance seen at RM4.70.

On the downside, the research house cautioned that a fall below the RM3.80 support level would invalidate the current bullish setup for Gamuda.

RHB said both counters are showing signs of sustained buying interest in the near term, supported by improving technical indicators and market momentum.

data.title: Stock Today: IOI Properties Dips 0.27% Amid Optimism On Singapore Re-Rating Story
data.description:
IOI Properties Group Bhd shares traded slightly lower at RM3.76 at the time of writing, down 0.27% or 1 sen, with intraday trading ranging between RM3.76 and RM3.96 and volume recorded at 101.48 million shares, as investor sentiment remained cautious despite renewed analyst optimism on its long-term asset monetisation strategy.

Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) maintained a BUY call on IOI Properties Group with a higher target price of RM5.20 versus RM4.15 previously, as analysts highlighted value unlocking from its Singapore assets, a structural upcycle in the Singapore office market and stronger dividend potential into FY27 as key drivers supporting a potential re-rating.

Analysts said the upcoming Malaysia REIT listing represents only the first phase of IOI Properties Group’s broader monetisation strategy, with attention expected to shift towards its Singapore portfolio, which is more than three times larger than its Malaysia REIT assets, once the listing is completed.

They noted that Singapore’s central business district office market is entering a structural upcycle supported by tight supply and strong demand, with rents at multi-year highs and vacancy levels at record lows. IOI Properties Group recently secured leasing at around SGD18 per square foot compared with a portfolio average of about SGD13 per square foot, signalling potential rental upside.

HLIB also outlined possible monetisation pathways for the Singapore assets, including a full REIT listing by CY29, a staged listing approach or earlier private fund monetisation by CY27, while improved rental reversion at key assets could serve as a medium-term catalyst.

Analysts added that IOI Properties Group’s China operations are gradually transitioning into a recurring income contributor, supported by early operational improvements at the Sheraton Grand Xiamen Hotel, which has reached EBITDA breakeven, and expectations of stronger footfall following a planned metro station by end-CY26.

HLIB also raised FY27 dividend expectations to as high as 21 sen per share, implying a yield of about 5.6%, supported by REIT proceeds and land sales, reinforcing the group’s appeal as a proxy to resilient Malaysia and Singapore property markets.
